sanskrit:
यत्तु प्रत्युपकारार्थं फलमुद्दिश्य वा पुनः।
दीयते च परिक्लिष्टं तद्दानं राजसं स्मृतम्॥
transliteration: yat tu pratyupakārārthaṃ phalam uddiśya vā punaḥ | dīyate ca parikliṣṭaṃ tad dānaṃ rājasaṃ smṛtam ||
literal_meaning: But that gift which is given for the sake of reciprocation, or again with an eye on the fruit, and is offered grudgingly—such charity is declared to be of the rajasic kind.
contextual_meaning: Giving that is secretly a deal, not a gift: it keeps score, expects pay-back, and leaves the giver tense or regretful. Psychologically it is an ego-inflation maneuver, not an act of care.
psychological_state: Controlled by the pleasure-anxiety cycle of the reward system; generosity is used to regulate self-esteem and avoid inner emptiness.
trigger_scenario: Donating to a colleague’s fundraiser only after checking if they’ll owe you a favor, then replaying the amount in your head all week.
